texto: What a nightmare!" cried Imtaz. "There's nothing gloomier than nature. You'll lose your sense of humor in the country. Unable to criticize the trees, your intelligence will lose its edge as you contemplate the plowed fields, and then, it'll be very easy for you to sing the praises of your fellow men because you won't be here to see and listen to them. Don't make that mistake. Never cut yourself off from mankind because, with distance, you're more likely to grant men extenuating circumstances. I love you too much to let you succumb to that weakness.
explicacao: Citação de Albert Cossery sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “What a nightmare!" cried Imtaz. "There's nothing gloomier than nature. You'll lose your sense of …” articula inteligencia, mankind, nature em registro de inteligencia, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: What a nightmare!" cried Imtaz. "There's nothing gloomier than nature. You'll lose your sense of humor in the country. Unable to criticize the trees, your intelligence will lose its edge as you contemplate the plowed fields, and then, it'll be very easy for you to sing the praises of your fellow men because you won't be here to see and listen to them. Don't make that mistake. Never cut yourself off from mankind because, with distance, you're more likely to grant men extenuating circumstances. I love you too much to let you succumb to that weakness.

texto: [T]he world we live in is governed by the most revolting bunch of crooks ever to defile the soil of this planet... [You] must never take them seriously, which is exactly what they want.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Albert Cossery sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “[T]he world we live in is governed by the most revolting bunch of crooks ever to defile the soil …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, lifestyle, power, útil em momentos de power e resistance.
